description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a somewhat flattened profile and a prominent central ridge running along its length. The surface is predominantly off-white to light gray, with subtle variations in tone and faint natural striations. One side features a slightly raised, rounded bulge, while the opposite side tapers to a more angular edge. The stone's texture appears rough and unpolished, with visible pitting and minor surface undulations. There is a handwritten marking in black ink on one face, reading 'PRI44909', suggesting cataloging or identification for urban or scientific use. The overall form is asymmetrical, with no clear geometric regularity, making it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban setting.
description: An irregular, off-white to light gray stone with a rough, pitted surface and a prominent central ridge, featuring a handwritten catalog number.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting the central ridge and the black handwritten marking.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the stone's thickness and the pronounced ridge, with less visibility of the catalog number.
description_view_2: The tapered, angular end of the stone is most visible here, showing the asymmetrical profile and subtle surface striations.
description_view_3: This view reveals the more rounded, bulging side of the stone, with pitting and undulations most apparent.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as an obstacle barrier
attribute_pattern: Subtle natural striations and pitting, with a handwritten catalog number on one face; no regular artificial patterning.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor pitting and surface undulations indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth man-made surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; the catalog number adds a human-made touch.
